PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-13-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jonathan Nightingale appeared by zoom for and with Linda Leppert (Petitioner) by zoom.
Robin White (Objector) appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.
Michael Richards, Susan Greene, Christine Rardin, Jenny Lowery, George Richards and Carol Richards also present by zoom.

Mr. Nightingale stated that the objector has been uncooperative, that she has been misinterpreting what is being said, and that he is here to make sure all of the heirs get what they are entitled to and being transparent. Ms. White stated that she has completely lost confidence in Mr. Nightingale's ability to act ethical, and to carry out the duties and responsibilities of her mother's estate. Ms. White also spoke of the family and percentages the heirs are entitled to, raised the issue of conflict, raised the issue of Mr. Nightingale wasting the asset by putting out the petition with a forged signature of hers, Linda being influenced by the aunt and uncle, the current petition not being clean, having the opportunity to sign a clean petition with actual signatures, etc. The court noted that all of the other parties are here today in support of Linda, raised the issue of limited authority with no bond and informed Mr. Nightingale that if it goes with full authority it will require bond. Mr. Nightingale stated that he would like full authority with a bond in the amount of $111,000.00. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration. Linda Leppert is appointed as the Administrator of the estate with full authority. Bond is set at $111,000.00. Status on close of estate is set for December 5, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: S Park. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-18-2021 Continued To Sep-15-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Jonathan Nightengale appeared by zoom for and with Linda Leppert by zoom.
Robin White Jones (Beneficiary) appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.
Michael Richards (Interested Party) appeared by zoom.

Ms. Jones stated that she objects to the granting of this petition and requested a 60-day continuance stating that she is looking for an Attorney. Mr. Nightingale objects to a continuance stating that there are six other shares of a property that need to be sold, that there is a 96-year old woman who needs money to fund for her care, that he had been communicating with Ms. Jones for many months and that Ms. Jones has known about this controversy for some time. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to September 15, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Jones to file her objections in writing no later than September 3, 2021. Any response to the objections must be filed by September 10, 2021.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robin Jones appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

Ms. Jones stated that as an equal intestate heir, she claims succession in interest to her Mother's 1/7 interest in the property, that on April 27, 2021 Mr. Nightingale filed a petition to determine succession to real property, that she never signed the petition and that her signature was forged. Ms. Jones also stated that she did not see the petition pre-filing, that Mr. Nightingale is not her Attorney, that she did not engage him as her Attorney and that Mr. nightingale never gave her an explanation as to how her signature was placed on any documents. In addition, Ms. Jones stated that she became aware of the petition because of her own research on the court's website, that she does not want to see this petition approved by the court, that Mr. Nightingale tried to coarse her to hire him and that Mr. Nightingale told her that if she did not sign on, he would file a more costly petition. Furthermore, Ms. Jones stated that on January 4, 2021 a false affidavit of joint tenants was recorded with the Recorder's Office in Richards estate, and that under California law the property could not be held in joint tenancy, but it could be held in tenants in common with no right of survivorship. The Court informed Ms. Jones that the issue regarding the deed was not before it. Further discussion was held regarding the appraisal amount of the Mother's interest and where that dollar figure came from, as well as the Aunt's interest being lower than the Mother's interest. The Petition to Determine Succession to Real Property is off calendar.

Judge Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.

PETITION TO DETERMINE SUCCESSION TO REAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar May-26-2021 Continued To Jun-28-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
